name: Catavento Cultural e Educacional, Sao Paulo
concept&appearance: Housed in the historic 'Palácio das Indústrias' (Palace of Industries), the museum is a landmark of Eclectic architecture designed by Domiziano Rossi and the office of Ramos de Azevedo. Built between 1911 and 1924, the building was originally intended to showcase São Paulo's industrial and agricultural progress. The exterior features exposed brickwork, imported metal structures visible in the attic, and elaborate decorative elements including sculptures of bulls (representing livestock) and dogs. The building resembles an Italian castle (specifically Castello Mackenzie in Genoa) and is situated in the Parque Dom Pedro II, serving as a vital project for the revitalization of the city's downtown area.
permanent_exhibitions: The museum is divided into four major sections: 1. Universe (Universo): Covers astronomy and the cosmos, featuring exhibits on the Solar System, meteorites, the interior of the Earth, and a replica of Neil Armstrong's footprint on the Moon. 2. Life (Vida): Focuses on biology and evolution, including the 'Dinos do Brasil' (Dinosaurs of Brazil) VR experience, a Butterfly Garden (Borboletário), and a 'Virtual Man' exhibit detailing the human body's systems (digestive, respiratory, etc.) alongside displays on Brazilian biomes and birds. 3. Ingenuity (Engenho): Dedicated to physics and human invention, featuring interactive mechanics, optics, and electromagnetism experiments, including a famous Van de Graaff generator that makes visitors' hair stand on end, as well as engines and fluid mechanics displays. 4. Society (Sociedade): Explores social and historical themes, including nanotechnology, chemistry, sustainability, environmental preservation, and drug prevention awareness.
central_hall: The museum features a majestic entrance hall characterized by a monumental staircase, which is a historical highlight where significant political figures have passed. The main hall space has high ceilings and integrates exhibits related to the human body, specifically the 'Virtual Man' display, connecting the historical grandeur of the palace with modern scientific interactivity.
special_effects_theaters: 1. Sala Dinos do Brasil: A virtual reality (VR) room where visitors use headsets to travel back to the Triassic and Cretaceous periods to see dinosaurs that inhabited Brazil. 2. Oceanário: An external dome structure with a 180-degree screen that projects immersive films about marine life and environmental preservation.
science_popularization_activities: The museum offers a robust educational program including the 'Nanoaventura' (interactive nanotechnology game), chemistry and fossil workshops, stop-motion animation activities, and kite-making classes. It actively hosts school groups with guided tours, teacher training programs, and special holiday events focusing on science education and environmental awareness.
